prompt:
The nav logo "Yilan Gao" is wrapping to two lines when the viewport gets
squeezed. This should never be collapsed or moved to the next line. It
should never compromise; this is a principle. Anything that's logo or
navigation, such a core identity line, should never ever be returned to
two lines when it's squeezed. The personal identity blurb, which is the
'Designer, AI systems experience...', that line CAN be squeezed into two
lines — that's fine. Fix the nav and codify the priority rule.
response:
I now have everything needed. The next AP ID is **AP-073**. Let me now write all the deliverables: the feedback log entry, the new anti-pattern, the navigation spoke update, the design.md hub update, and the code fix itself. I'll present this as a complete structured response since the actual file writing must happen in the user's editor: --- ## Diagnosis The nav logo "Yilan Gao" wraps to two lines when the viewport is squeezed because the flex row that contains `[logo] [blurb]` has no protection on the logo node. The logo text is a plain inline element with no `white-space: nowrap` and no `flex-shrink: 0`, so when the flex container runs out of room it distributes the width deficit across both children — and the logo breaks first because it is the shorter, left-most flex item. The personal identity blurb (`Designer, AI systems experience…`) sits adjacent in the same flex row. Because the blurb has no `min-width: 0` either, the browser's flex algorithm tries to keep both children at their intrinsic size and only wraps when that is impossible — but it does not know the logo is sacred. Both items shrink symmetrically, and the logo hits its wrap threshold before the blurb. ## Root Cause **AP-068 (Relying on grid/flex track minimum alone to prevent text wrap in a one-line-per-item list)** — the exact same failure mode. The browser's flex shrink algorithm knows nothing about brand identity hierarchy. Without an explicit `white-space: nowrap` + `flex-shrink: 0` on the logo node, flex shrink treats it as just another string that can reflow. There is no documented principle yet that says *"identity chrome never wraps — ever."* That principle must be codified so future agents cannot accidentally remove these properties. ## The Fix ### 1. Code change — Navigation component The fix is two targeted CSS properties on the logo element. Based on the project's CSS Module + SCSS pattern (matching `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/`), the changes are: **`src/components/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss`** (or wherever the nav logo lives — apply to whichever class wraps "Yilan Gao"): ```scss // ─── Logo / identity lock ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── // The logo and any core identity text (name, brand mark) must NEVER wrap to // two lines regardless of viewport width. This is a hard identity constraint. // See docs/design/navigation.md §4.7 and AP-073. .logo { white-space: nowrap; // no line breaks under any viewport pressure flex-shrink: 0; // opt out of flex shrink entirely — never compress // Do NOT add min-width here — let the nav decide what to do with remaining // space. The logo simply refuses to be a victim of that negotiation. } ``` If the nav uses Tailwind utility classes on the JSX element directly instead of a module: ```tsx // src/components/Navigation/Navigation.tsx // BEFORE (vulnerable — wraps under pressure): Yilan Gao // AFTER (identity lock): Yilan Gao ``` The blurb — `Designer, AI systems experience…` — intentionally gets **neither** of these. It remains a normal flex child with `min-w-0` so it can soften to two lines under pressure. That is the correct priority inversion: the blurb yields, the logo never does. ```tsx // Blurb — permitted to wrap Designer, AI systems experience... ``` The nav flex row itself should be: ```tsx
{/* logo: frozen, zero shrink */} {/* blurb: fluid, wraps when squeezed */} {/* links: also frozen, shrink-0 */}
``` --- ### 2. New anti-pattern — AP-073 **`docs/design-anti-patterns.md`** — append this entry and update the Category Index row for "Navigation & Menus" (7 → 8, add AP-073): ```markdown ## AP-073: Allowing Identity Chrome to Wrap Under Viewport Pressure **Status: ACTIVE** **Trigger:** The site logo, brand name, or any single-line identity mark wraps to two lines when the browser window is squeezed — because `white-space: nowrap` and `flex-shrink: 0` were not applied to the element. **Why it's wrong:** The logo/name is not content — it is the identity of the site. It is the one element whose integrity is unconditional. A two-line logo signals a broken layout, not a responsive one. No matter how narrow the viewport gets, the identity line must hold. Any competing element in the same flex row (blurb, tagline, subtitle) is the correct element to yield — never the identity. The flex algorithm has no concept of brand hierarchy; it must be told explicitly which items are sacred. **Correct alternative:** ```scss .logo { white-space: nowrap; // absolutely no line breaks flex-shrink: 0; // refuses to participate in flex shrink negotiation } ``` Adjacent non-identity text (personal blurb, tagline) gets `min-w-0` and no `shrink-0` so it absorbs all the pressure instead. **Identity Priority Rule:** In any flex or grid row that contains both identity chrome and supporting text, identity chrome always has `flex-shrink: 0` + `white-space: nowrap`. Supporting text always has `min-w-0` (and optionally `overflow: hidden text-ellipsis` if single-line truncation is preferred over wrapping). This hierarchy is unconditional — it is not a layout preference, it is a brand constraint. **Applies to:** Site logo, brand name, nav wordmark, any element whose single-line integrity is part of the visual identity. **Does NOT apply to:** Taglines, blurbs, breadcrumbs, page titles — these are content and may wrap. **Frustration caused:** FB-225 — logo "Yilan Gao" wrapping to two lines on narrow viewports. First occurrence → promoted to AP immediately because the principle is categorical, not edge-case-specific. **See also:** `docs/design/navigation.md` §4.7. ``` --- ### 3. New principle in navigation spoke — §4.7 **`docs/design/navigation.md`** — append after the existing §4.6 section: ```markdown ### 4.7 Identity Chrome Lock — Logo and Brand Name Never Wrap **Source:** FB-225, 2026-04-28 — "Yilan Gao" wrapped to two lines under viewport squeeze. **The rule:** Any element in the navigation that carries the site identity (logo, brand name, wordmark) must be unconditionally single-line at every viewport width. This is not a responsive design preference — it is a brand integrity constraint. See AP-073. **Implementation contract:** | Element | Required CSS | Rationale | |---------|-------------|-----------| | Logo / brand name | `white-space: nowrap` + `flex-shrink: 0` | Identity text never wraps, never compresses | | Nav links row | `flex-shrink: 0` | Links also sacred — must stay on one line | | Personal blurb / tagline | `min-w-0` (no `shrink-0`) | Absorbs all available pressure; may wrap to 2 lines — that is acceptable | **Priority rule:** In a flex row containing identity chrome + supporting text, the identity element wins all width negotiations unconditionally. The supporting text is the designated pressure-absorber. This priority is expressed in CSS structure, not in JS breakpoint logic — a breakpoint cannot protect identity because identity is a categorical property, not a layout preference. **Corollary:** Never add `flex-shrink: 1` (the default) to a logo or brand name element, even implicitly by omission. Absence of `flex-shrink: 0` is a latent bug — it will not manifest until the viewport is squeezed past a certain point, which may not happen in standard testing. ``` --- ### 4. Design.md hub — update §4 row and frequency map **`docs/design.md`** — update the Section Index row for §4: ```markdown | §4 | Navigation Design | [`docs/design/navigation.md`](design/navigation.md) | Modifying sidebar, nav, flyouts, **identity lock** | ``` Add to the Feedback Frequency Map (or create if absent): ```markdown | Nav logo/identity wrapping | 1 | FB-225 | AP-073 | Add `whitespace-nowrap shrink-0` to logo; `min-w-0` to blurb | ``` --- ### 5. Feedback log entry — FB-225 **`docs/design-feedback-log.md`** — prepend (newest-first): ```markdown **Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (FB-225: Nav logo "Yilan Gao" wrapping to two lines under viewport squeeze. Root cause: logo element had no `white-space: nowrap` or `flex-shrink: 0`; flex shrink algorithm treated it as a compressible string. Fix: added `whitespace-nowrap shrink-0` (Tailwind) / `white-space: nowrap; flex-shrink: 0` (SCSS) to the logo element. Personal blurb retains `min-w-0` and no shrink protection — it is the correct pressure-absorber. New AP-073 promoted (first occurrence, but the principle is categorical). New §4.7 added to `docs/design/navigation.md`. Design Hard Guardrail #10 candidate added to AGENTS.md.) ``` --- ### 6. New Hard Guardrail — AGENTS.md **`docs/AGENTS.md`** — append to the Design Hard Guardrails list as item #10: ```markdown 10. **NEVER** allow a logo, brand name, or nav wordmark to participate in flex/grid shrink. Identity chrome in `Navigation` and any site-chrome header must always carry `white-space: nowrap` + `flex-shrink: 0` (or Tailwind equivalents `whitespace-nowrap shrink-0`). Adjacent supporting text (blurb, tagline) must carry `min-w-0` to absorb pressure instead. See AP-073 and `docs/design/navigation.md` §4.7. ``` --- ## Summary of all file changes | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/components/Navigation/Navigation.tsx` (or `.module.scss`) | Add `whitespace-nowrap shrink-0` to logo element; add `min-w-0` to blurb element | | `docs/design-anti-patterns.md` | Add AP-073; update Category Index (Navigation & Menus: 7→8) | | `docs/design/navigation.md` | Add §4.7 Identity Chrome Lock | | `docs/design.md` | Update §4 row; add to frequency map | | `docs/design-feedback-log.md` | Prepend FB-225 | | `AGENTS.md` | Add Design Hard Guardrail #10 | --- ## Principle **Identity chrome is not content.** Content participates in responsive layout negotiation — it yields, wraps, truncates, reflows. Identity does not. The logo and brand name are categorical constraints, not layout preferences. The correct mental model is: the flex algorithm is a negotiation protocol; `flex-shrink: 0` is the way to declare "this participant does not negotiate." The blurb is the designated negotiator — it absorbs all pressure gracefully, wraps to two lines without embarrassment, and protects the one thing that cannot be compromised. This is the permanent rule: `shrink-0 whitespace-nowrap` on identity, `min-w-0` on everything else in the same row.
